title: Fix Ruby scanner P2 bugs: unicode truncation in heredoc + missing MarkEnd() + test gaps
description:
P2 bugs in scanners/ruby/scanner.go:

1. P2.3: scanHeredocWord truncates non-ASCII to byte via byte(lexer.Lookahead). Fix: use utf8.EncodeRune (same fix already applied to Bash scanner). Affects both quoted (line ~648) and unquoted (lines ~654, ~657) heredoc word loops.

2. P2.6: Serialize stores openDelimiter/closeDelimiter as single byte, truncating int32 values above 0xFF. Should use multi-byte encoding.

3. P2.2: Dead advance() call at EOF in scanLiteralContent — harmless but confusing.

4. P2.4: Missing EOF guard in scanShortInterpolation before ContainsRune check.

5. P3.1: Missing MarkEnd() before return true in operator disambiguation paths (BlockAmpersand, all star/minus variants, ElementReferenceBracket). Consistent with other scanners but not robust.

6. TEST GAP: Zero tests for scanHeredocContent (most complex function in the scanner). Also no nested delimiter tests, no EOF-during-literal tests.
